meta.title: TechPulse
meta.tagline: Tech & AI news, hot takes, and who's saying what — from the builders you follow
meta.note: Built from your X Following timeline, filtered to tech/AI builders by profile. Click any story to see every take and what different people are saying.

stories.title: AI Agents Are Actually Doing Real Things Now
stories.summary: Builders share wild moments where AI agents autonomously navigate UIs, sign up for services, and execute real-world tasks — the agentic era feels like it just clicked into place.

takes.name: dax
takes.text:
i told opencode to use my browser to sign up for telnyx and give itself a number

they had agent instructions for sign up! it even had this crazy bot challenge

the rest of the onboarding was tailored for my agent and it got a number and got everything setup https://t.co/2VANBeox4T

takes.name: dex
takes.text:
sunday watching - criminally underviewed agentic coding advice from Segment cofounder and former OpenAI MTS on Codex @calvinfo 

https://t.co/jPo18j7Pnd

takes.name: dex
takes.text: If you’re building a software factory, your goal shouldn’t be to fully remove engineers from the workflow - @vaibcode is one of the best in the business when it comes to designing agentic workflows - great insights when it comes to where human judgement is still important in https://t.co/3eb1xhGLg9

takes.name: Mike Futia
takes.text:
I just built a Meta Ad Comment Spy in Claude Code that turns every comment on your ads into a voice-of-customer goldmine 🤯

Point Comment Spy at your ad account → it pulls every comment and reply, clusters them into objections, questions, and proof, and writes your next 5 ad https://t.co/xot1EUQ6sl

takes.name: James Perkins
takes.text:
Time to experiment with a small portion of my investments.

Claude + Robinhood agentic trading... What could go wrong :)

stories.title: Everyone Has AI Now — So What's the Real Moat?
stories.summary: The AI advantage is expiring fast. Builders debate what differentiates you in 2026 when everyone can code, design, and create with AI. Distribution? Taste? Speed?

takes.name: Thomas Trimoreau
takes.text:
- AI writes better code.
- AI designs better interfaces.
- AI creates better content.

If that's true...

What are humans actually better at right now ?

takes.name: Thomas Trimoreau
takes.text:
Everyone is having AI now.

That advantage expired fast.

What's the new advantage?

takes.name: Thomas Trimoreau
takes.text:
2010: I need a team of 50 to build this
2026: I need a laptop and Claude
2030: ...

takes.name: Can Vardar
takes.text: distribution is the most important thing you can imagine. without it, even the best product is invisible. with it, mediocre ideas win

takes.name: Layton Gott
takes.text:
Everyone keeps asking who wins the AI race…

Anthropic or OpenAI?

The answer at this point is neither.

It’s the government. (for frontier models at least)

They can pull a closed model offline whenever they want. 

Which is ultimately the same reason open source will https://t.co/BMqEnuPkhQ

takes.name: George Ten
takes.text:
AI can’t eat info products in markets that don’t want AI (wink wink).

That’s one solution.

Obviously doesn’t fit everyone.

stories.title: Weekend Drop: Terminal Slides, WSL Backends, Figma Shaders
stories.summary: A wave of indie releases hit this weekend — a terminal-based Markdown presenter, WSL backend support in Nightly, Figma shader demos, and a new browser extension published.

takes.name: nexxel
takes.text:
i wanted presentations but in the terminal

so i’m building talktui

write markdown and present it straight from your terminal

layouts, themes, fragments, code blocks, mermaid diagrams, tables, images, etc

built using opentui https://t.co/7VzlhUrDkg

takes.name: Julius
takes.text:
Kinda offline this weekend but forgot to announce that WSL backends are available on Nightly! 

AIming to release new latest tag tomorrow. Last week was just a lot of chaos. Lot of goodies on Nightly now!!! https://t.co/ga6nZcXlMe

takes.name: Praveen Kumar
takes.text: Made with @figma shaders https://t.co/q1F46NQ2wt

takes.name: vimtor
takes.text: the first version has been published https://t.co/26QKO9r5rX

takes.name: Ridd 🤿
takes.text: love the @interaction loading state as it builds you a website https://t.co/X7lU4YpdXh

takes.name: Steve Ruiz
takes.text: test this please https://t.co/Khdk4mrWgM

stories.title: LLMs Hit a 'Dumb Zone' Around 120K Tokens
stories.summary: Matt Pocock shares a trick to feel the context collapse — use /rewind to replay when your agent did something dumb at ~120K tokens. Kent C. Dodds wonders if Cursor's auto-compaction quietly solves it.

takes.name: Matt Pocock
takes.text:
Here's a way to get a visceral sense of the dumb zone (starts at ~120K tokens on SOTA models):

1. Agent does something dumb
2. /rewind to before the stupid thing
3. /compact (assuming you're in a spot where compacting makes sense)
4. Run it again

Watch it get smarter

takes.name: Kent C. Dodds 🏹
takes.text:
@mattpocockuk How do you feel about automatic compaction? I think cursor's must be great because I rarely feel like the model gets stupider as conversation length grows. 

Would really love to see some research to know whether I'm just oblivious or if @cursor_ai really is just good at this

takes.name: Matt Pocock
takes.text:
Imagine I have /skill-1 and /skill-2, both with disable-model-invocation: true

I tell Claude Code: "/skill-1, /skill-2 do XYZ"

Which of the skills will be invoked?

takes.name: Matt Pocock
takes.text: I'll give you a clue - the behaviour was one thing 3 weeks ago, another thing 1 week ago, and back to the original today.

takes.name: Matt Pocock
takes.text:
~3 weeks ago: /skill-1 only
~1 week ago: /skill-1 and skill-2
Today: /skill-1 only

IMO invoking both skills is the correct behavior - the user well mentioned them!

cc @delba_oliveira I assume they have given you infinite power already

stories.title: rauchg: Ditch LinkedIn, Own a Domain, Ship a Real Page
stories.summary: Guillermo Rauch dropped two viral takes: you need a website page linking to your work, not a LinkedIn — and buying a clean domain for your family's email is peak internet joy.

takes.name: Guillermo Rauch
takes.text: You don't need a LinkedIn, you need a page on your website describing and linking to what you shipped

takes.name: Guillermo Rauch
takes.text: You need a Link, not a LinkedIn 😂

takes.name: Guillermo Rauch
takes.text: I now own https://t.co/I2grp6UkeV. Excited to give my children clean email handles. Domains are awesome. The internet is awesome.

takes.name: Evil Rabbit
takes.text: ▲ Messi is joining Vercel as Design Engineer. https://t.co/lS4932s3en

stories.title: Next.js Drama Refuses to Die in 2026
stories.summary: Devs are still fighting about Next.js — the React core team is hosting coffee meetups about it, and at least one dev has gone full 'just write your own React framework.'

takes.name: dax
takes.text: i can't believe we got fighting about nextjs in 2026

takes.name: Tommy (in SF from July 6)
takes.text:
@thdxr everyone should just make their own React framework like me

https://t.co/Tq036inEO2

takes.name: Andrew Clark
takes.text: New York: Come join the Next.js team tomorrow morning for coffee and bagels! We can talk about 16.3, or anything else you’re interested in https://t.co/TwZl6n5Oxm

stories.title: Is Anthropic Hardware-Mogging OpenAI?
stories.summary: Builders notice Anthropic may be winning a quiet hardware race, while some quietly admit switching to Codex for daily coding. Favorite AI benchmark: 'did the govt care enough to restrict it?'

takes.name: Can Vardar
takes.text: i think anthropic is hardwaremogging openai and sam altman needs to start hardwaremaxxing sooner or later

takes.name: Can Vardar
takes.text: i regret to admit i have switched to codex

takes.name: Can Vardar
takes.text: funniest benchmark in ai is still “did the govt care enough to restrict it”

stories.title: Selling Agent Templates Will Eat the Entire Market
stories.summary: Riley Brown's spicy claim: agent template marketplaces will cannibalize the software industry. The nuance: the best software factories amplify great engineers, not replace them.

takes.name: Riley Brown
takes.text: Selling agent templates will eat this ENTIRE market.

takes.name: dex
takes.text: If you’re building a software factory, your goal shouldn’t be to fully remove engineers from the workflow - @vaibcode is one of the best in the business when it comes to designing agentic workflows - great insights when it comes to where human judgement is still important in https://t.co/3eb1xhGLg9

stories.title: GitHub Copilot Releases Agentic Harness Benchmarks
stories.summary: GitHub published comparative data: Copilot's agentic harness vs. native model harnesses on SWE-bench, model and task held fixed. Also shipped a /impeccable design-refresh skill.

takes.name: GitHub
takes.text:
We benchmarked the GitHub Copilot agentic harness against the harnesses that ship leading models natively.

Holding the model and task fixed across SWE-bench Verified, SWE-bench Pro, SkillsBench, TerminalBench, and Win-Hill, the results were clear:
✅ Task resolution on par with https://t.co/Pfu2nN3Son

takes.name: GitHub
takes.text:
Using the /impeccable skill built into the GitHub Copilot app, @cassidoo gave her PocketCal app a design refresh! ✨

Enable in Settings > Experimental to try it out.
https://t.co/mmBbvB1P9P https://t.co/jvva9ciAUh

stories.title: freeCodeCamp Drops Free MLOps + Local LLM Courses
stories.summary: freeCodeCamp launched free courses on MLOps with MLflow and running local LLMs for privacy-sensitive use cases — practical AI infrastructure education, no tuition required.

takes.name: freeCodeCamp.org
takes.text:
MLOps helps you take machine learning models from experimentation to production.

In this course, you'll learn how to use MLflow to manage the entire ML lifecycle.

You'll also learn Databricks and Hugging Face to build reproducible, scalable & observable systems. https://t.co/DSPwQkmYrl

takes.name: freeCodeCamp.org
takes.text:
Cloud-based LLMs are powerful.

But there are times when local models are a better choice, especially when you're dealing with sensitive info.

Here, Manoj shows you how to use Ollama, Python, and LangChain to run LLMs locally and build privacy-first, AI-powered apps. https://t.co/JT8hQH28S3
